meaning_kannada: ಕ್ರಿಯೆಯು ನಡೆಯುವ ವಿಧಾನವನ್ನು ತಿಳಿಸುವ ಕ್ರಿಯಾವಿಶೇಷಣಗಳು (Slowly, Quickly, Beautifully).
meaning_english:
Answers the question 'How?'. Most adverbs of manner end in '-ly'.

• Rule: Adjectives are modified into adverbs by adding '-ly'.
• Example: She walks *slowly*. He writes *beautifully*.
• Exception: Fast, Hard, Well (Do not write 'fastly' or 'hardly' for manner).

meaning_kannada: ಇಂಗ್ಲಿಷ್ ವ್ಯಾಕರಣದಲ್ಲಿ 'Fastly' ಎಂಬ ಯಾವುದೇ ಪದವಿಲ್ಲ. 'Fast' ಪದವೇ ವಿಶೇಷಣ ಮತ್ತು ಕ್ರಿಯಾವಿಶೇಷಣ ಎರಡೂ ಆಗಿ ಕೆಲಸ ಮಾಡುತ್ತದೆ.
meaning_english:
The word 'fastly' does not exist in English grammar. 'Fast' acts as both an adjective and an adverb.

• Wrong: He runs *fastly*.
• Right: He runs *fast*. ✅
• Right: He is a *fast* runner (Adjective).

meaning_kannada: ಕ್ರಿಯೆಯು ಎಷ್ಟು ಬಾರಿ ನಡೆಯುತ್ತದೆ ಎಂದು ತಿಳಿಸುವ ಪದಗಳ (Always, Never, Often) ಸರಿಯಾದ ಸ್ಥಾನ.
meaning_english:
Adverbs of frequency are placed BEFORE the main verb, but AFTER the 'be' verbs (is, am, are, was, were).

• Structure 1 (Before Main Verb): He *always* comes late.
• Structure 2 (After Be-Verb): He is *always* late.

meaning_kannada: 'Hard' ಎಂದರೆ ಕಷ್ಟಪಟ್ಟು (Positive), ಆದರೆ 'Hardly' ಎಂದರೆ 'ಇಲ್ಲವೇ ಇಲ್ಲ' ಅಥವಾ ಅಷ್ಟಕಷ್ಟೆ (Negative) ಎಂದರ್ಥ.
meaning_english:
'Hard' means with great energy/effort. 'Hardly' means scarcely or almost not at all.

• Example 1: He worked *hard* to clear the UPSC prelims. (Effort)
• Example 2: He *hardly* studied for the exam, so he failed. (Almost zero study).

meaning_kannada: 'Late' ಎಂದರೆ ತಡವಾಗಿ ಎಂದರ್ಥ, ಆದರೆ 'Lately' ಎಂದರೆ ಇತ್ತೀಚೆಗೆ (Recently) ಎಂದರ್ಥ.
meaning_english:
'Late' refers to arriving after the expected time. 'Lately' means recently.

• Example 1: He arrived *late* for his work shift.
• Example 2: I haven't seen him *lately* (recently).

meaning_kannada: 'Too' ಪದವು ಸಾಮಾನ್ಯವಾಗಿ ಅಗತ್ಯಕ್ಕಿಂತ ಹೆಚ್ಚು ಎಂಬ ನಕಾರಾತ್ಮಕ (Negative) ಅರ್ಥವನ್ನು ನೀಡುತ್ತದೆ. ಇದು 'too + adjective + to + verb' ರಚನೆ ಹೊಂದುತ್ತದೆ.
meaning_english:
'Too' indicates excess and has a negative connotation. The structure 'too... to' implies that something cannot happen.

• Example: He is *too* weak *to* walk. (He is so weak that he cannot walk).
• Tip: Do not use 'too' in place of 'very' for pleasant adjectives (e.g., say 'very happy', not 'too happy').

meaning_kannada: 'Enough' ಪದವು ಕ್ರಿಯಾವಿಶೇಷಣವಾದಾಗ ಅದು ಗುಣವಾಚಕದ (Adjective) ನಂತರವೇ ಬರಬೇಕು.
meaning_english:
When 'Enough' acts as an adverb, it must be placed directly AFTER the adjective it modifies.

• Wrong: He is *enough wise* to handle the accounts ledger.
• Right: He is *wise enough* to handle the accounts ledger. ✅

meaning_kannada: ಒಂದೇ ವಾಕ್ಯದಲ್ಲಿ ಹತ್ತಾರು ಕ್ರಿಯಾವಿಶೇಷಣಗಳು ಒಟ್ಟಿಗೆ ಬಂದರೆ ಮೊದಲು Manner, ನಂತರ Place, ಕೊನೆಯಲ್ಲಿ Time ಬರಬೇಕು.
meaning_english:
When multiple adverbs modify a single verb, the standard sequence is Manner (How) -> Place (Where) -> Time (When).

• Wrong: She danced yesterday (Time) beautifully (Manner) hall inside (Place).
• Right: She danced *beautifully* (Manner) *in the hall* (Place) *yesterday* (Time). ✅

meaning_kannada: ವಾಕ್ಯವು ನಕಾರಾತ್ಮಕ ಕ್ರಿಯಾವಿಶೇಷಣಗಳಿಂದ (Hardly, Seldom, Scarcely, Never) ಆರಂಭವಾದಾಗ ಕರ್ತೃವಿಗಿಂತ ಮುಂಚೆ ಸಹಾಯಕರ ಕ್ರಿಯಾಪದ ಬರಬೇಕು.
meaning_english:
When a sentence begins with negative adverbs, inversion occurs: the auxiliary verb comes before the subject.

• Wrong: Seldom he goes to the office late.
• Right: Seldom *does he go* to the office late. ✅

meaning_kannada: 'Seldom or never' ಅಥವಾ 'Seldom, if ever' ಎಂಬುದು ಸ್ಥಿರ ನುಡಿಗಟ್ಟು. ಇದನ್ನು ಅದಲು-ಬದಲು ಮಾಡಬಾರದು.
meaning_english:
The correct idiomatic phrases are 'Seldom or never' and 'Seldom, if ever'. Examiners often mix them up to trap you.

• Wrong: He seldom or ever visits his hometown.
• Right: He *seldom or never* visits his hometown. ✅
• Right: He *seldom, if ever* visits his hometown. ✅
